layers.title: Register logs command
layers.summary: The CLI command registry now includes a new logs module alongside the existing worker lifecycle commands. The new module validates a host:port worker address and positive line count, checks for a matching worker process by its -worker_address argument, then invokes tail on the worker log file under the local log directory.
layers.diff:
diff --git a/cli/command.py b/cli/command.py
@@ -150,6 +150,7 @@ def main():
         "stop",
         "up",
         "down",
+        "logs",
         "runscript",
         "generate_helm_chart",
         "generate_cpp_template",
diff --git a/cli/logs.py b/cli/logs.py
@@ -0,0 +1,109 @@
+class Command(BaseCommand):
+    """Print the last N lines of a worker's log file to stdout."""
+
+    name = "logs"
+    description = "print recent log lines for a worker service"
+
+    def add_arguments(self, parser):
+        parser.add_argument(
+            "-w", "--worker_address", metavar="ADDR", required=True,
+            help="address (host:port) of the worker whose log to print",
+        )
+        parser.add_argument(
+            "-n", "--lines", metavar="N", default=_DEFAULT_LINES, type=_parse_positive_int,
+            help="number of trailing log lines to print",
+        )

layers.title: Cover log tail behavior
layers.summary: Tests exercise the new command after the CLI implementation layer is in place. They mock process lookup and tail execution for the success path, and verify malformed worker addresses or line counts are rejected before shelling out.
layers.diff:
diff --git a/cli/tests/test_logs.py b/cli/tests/test_logs.py
@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+class TestLogsCommand(unittest.TestCase):
+    """Tests for :class:`yr.datasystem.cli.logs.Command`."""
+
+    @mock.patch("yr.datasystem.cli.logs.subprocess.run")
+    @mock.patch.object(logs.Command, "find_worker_pid", return_value=4321)
+    def test_run_prints_recent_lines(self, _mock_pid, mock_run):
+        mock_run.return_value = mock.Mock(returncode=0, stdout="a\nb\nc\n", stderr="")
+
+        cmd = logs.Command()
+        args = mock.Mock(worker_address="127.0.0.1:31501", lines=100)
+        rc = cmd.run(args)
+
+        self.assertEqual(rc, logs.BaseCommand.SUCCESS)
+        mock_run.assert_called_once_with(
+            ["tail", "-n", "100", "/opt/yuanrong/datasystem/log/127.0.0.1:31501.log"],
+            capture_output=True, text=True,
+        )

layers.title: Document operator workflow
layers.summary: The docs layer explains the operator-facing intent behind the command and the implementation plan that matches the code. It describes the read-only local scope, address-based worker selection, default tail length, and explicitly excludes follow mode, aggregation, and filtering.
layers.diff:
diff --git a/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-15-dscli-logs.md b/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-15-dscli-logs.md
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+# `dscli logs` Subcommand — Implementation Plan
+
+1. **Add `cli/logs.py`** — a `Command(BaseCommand)` subclass:
+   - `name`/`description`, and `add_arguments` for `-w/--worker_address`
+     (required) and `-n/--lines` (default 100).
+2. **Register the command** — add `"logs"` to the `modules` list in
+   `cli/command.py`.
diff --git a/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-15-dscli-logs-design.md b/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-15-dscli-logs-design.md
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+Add a read-only `dscli logs` subcommand that prints the last N lines of a
+datasystem worker's log file, selected by the worker's address.
+
+**In scope:**
+- A `logs` subcommand: `dscli logs -w <host:port> [-n N]`.
+- Output: the last N lines of the worker's log on stdout (default N = 100).
+- Local host only; read-only (no mutation of worker state).

summary: This PR adds a read-only dscli logs subcommand that tails a local worker log by worker address, with tests and design documentation.
